steps.title: Locate the needed comparison
steps.content: The student wants evidence that bufadienolide does not fully explain tadpole egg preferences. The strongest support must compare eggs that both lack bufadienolide but were eaten at different rates.

steps.title: Use the native-species contrast
steps.content: Striped burrowing frog eggs and dainty green tree frog eggs are both native amphibian eggs, so neither produces bufadienolide. The tadpoles ate 10% of the striped burrowing frog eggs but only 1% of the dainty green tree frog eggs. B only notes survival rates, C compares a bufadienolide-producing species with a nonproducing species, and D shows no preference difference. The answer is A.
